clinical_history: 70 year-old woman with type II diabetes mellitus presented to emergency room with complaint of chest pain.  What is the diagnosis?
diagnosis: The rhythm is sinus at about 65 bpm.  ST elevations are present in leads II, III and aVF and V4-V6 with ST segment depressions in lead I, aVR, aVL and VI-V2.  These findings are consistent with acute inferior-lateral wall (and possibly posterior) myocardial infarction. Cardiac enzymes were elevated.  The patient was taken to the cardiac catheterization laboratory and found to have a totally obstructed left anterior descending vessel;  a 20~ stenosis of the left main; the left circumflex system had 60-70 ~ stenoses and the right coronary artery had a 95~ sub-total occlusion of its mid-portion with distal thrombus. Echocardiogram showed a normal global left ventricular ejection fraction with moderate inferior hypokinesis. The patient underwent successful emergency coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG) for multivessel disease.
